Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Team South Africa suffer devastating 4x100m relay injury blow

South Africa’s hopes of a medal in the men’s 4x100m relay suffered a devastating injury blow on Monday evening.

South Africa’s hopes of a medal in the men’s 4x100m relay suffered a devastating injury blow on Monday evening.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

Benjamin Richardson, who would’ve been a key member of the four-man squad, limped across the line in his heat in the 200m with an upper leg injury.

Richardson finished in last place in51.86, putting him in serious doubt for a place in the Team South Africa quartet.

Just 24 hours earlier, Richardson posted an impressive 9.95 in the semi-finals of the men’s 100m, narrowly missing out on a place in the final.

It remains to be seen what the coaching staff elect to do to fill the void.

The heats for the men’s 4x100m relay will take place onThursday, 8 Augustat11:10and11:19(SA time) with the final scheduled forFriday, 9 Augustat19:30(SA time).

Those selected for the South African 4x100m relay squad were: Shaun Maswangani, Bradley Nkoana, Benjamin Richardson, Akani Simbine, Bayanda Walaza, withSineseipho Dambile listed as a reserve.

Meanwhile,Wayde van Niekerkreached the semi-finals of the men’s200mat theParis Olympics 2024on Monday evening.

However, it wasn’t plain sailing for the star South African track star.

Van Niekerk finished third in his heat in a pedestrian20.42.

That time wound up being theslowesttime recorded by the 18 athletes who automatically qualified for the semi-finals.

Meanwhile,Shaun Maswanganialso finished third in his heat, but in a quicker20.20to also reach the semi-final stage.

However, there was heartbreak for South Africa’s third entrantBenjamin Richardsonwho appeared to pull a hamstring in his heat and limped across the line in last place in51.86.

The semi-finals will take place onWednesday, 7 Augustfrom20:02(SA time).

South Africa started Monday with four medals with Tatjana Smith (gold and silver) and the men’s sevens rugby and Alan Hatherly (bronze) putting them in a tie for 29th place on the overall table.

title: Team South Africa suffer devastating 4x100m relay injury blow
author: Garrin Lambley
description: South Africa's hopes of a medal in the men's 4x100m relay suffered a devastating injury blow on Monday evening.
